entities.description: gradient based entity resolution is a method used to evaluate the quality of a constructed knowledge graph  kg
basic kg construction is a standard practice using simple exact name matching for entity merging
figure 6 presents the comparative results of the evaluation between the two methods
entity count is a metric used to measure the number of entities in the graph
density is a metric used to measure the connectivity of the graph

entities.entity_name: diameter of the largest connected component
entities.description: diameter of the largest connected component is a metric measuring the longest shortest path in the largest connected part of the graph
number of connected components is a metric counting the separate parts of the graph
the basic baseline serves as the standard for comparison in the evaluation of the gradient based er method
the er module is the component responsible for identifying conceptual entities with different names
12  is the percentage reduction in the number of entities achieved by the gradient based er method
20  is the percentage increase in graph density achieved by the gradient based er method across datasets
many graph based methods are a group of techniques that employ simple exact name matching for entity merging
datasets are the collections of data used to evaluate the performance of the gradient based er method
graph reasoning is a task facilitated by the improved connectivity of the resulting graphs

relations.description: gradient based entity resolution is compared against basic kg construction to evaluate quality
figure 6 presents the results of the comparison involving gradient based entity resolution
gradient based entity resolution reduces the entity count by 12  compared to the baseline
gradient based entity resolution boosts graph density by over 20  across datasets

relations.tgt_entity_name: diameter of the largest connected component
relations.description: gradient based entity resolution reduces the diameter of the largest connected component  indicating a more compact graph
gradient based entity resolution reduces the number of connected components  mitigating fragmentation
the er module is the specific component of gradient based entity resolution that identifies entities
gradient based entity resolution is evaluated against the basic baseline to demonstrate optimization
the gradient based er method achieves a 12  reduction in entity count
the gradient based er method achieves a boost of over 20  in graph density
basic kg construction is standard practice in many graph based methods
the gradient based er method s performance is evaluated across multiple datasets
the structural improvements from gradient based entity resolution facilitate better graph reasoning
